summary: File-level verification of supabase migration 006_b8_verification.sql. The is_admin() overload-ambiguity fix is correct and the migration is well-formed and idempotent. 17/17 pytest checks pass. `flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos` reports 0 errors, 0 warnings, 77 info-level deprecations (accepted by user).
critical_code_review_comments: Migration 006 line 33 has `drop function if exists public.is_admin();` BEFORE any table / policy / RPC DDL (first DDL is `create table` on line 36). This is the correct ordering — the drop runs before Postgres has to resolve any policy USING (public.is_admin()) clause.

Exactly two `create function public.is_admin(...)` definitions exist across the migrations dir: 001 (1-arg with default auth.uid()) and 005 (0-arg). After 006 drops the 0-arg variant, only 001's version remains, so every no-arg call site `public.is_admin()` resolves uniquely.

All 10 `public.is_admin()` call sites inside 005's RPC bodies (admin_log, admin_suspend_user, admin_unsuspend_user, admin_soft_delete_user, admin_restore_user, admin_resolve_report, admin_dismiss_report, admin_set_verification, admin_dashboard_metrics, admin_user_summary) are zero-arg and use `create or replace function`, so Postgres re-resolves at execute time — no re-run of 005 needed after 006.

006 is fully idempotent: CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX use IF NOT EXISTS; every CREATE FUNCTION uses OR REPLACE; every CREATE POLICY has a preceding DROP POLICY IF EXISTS with matching name/table; storage.buckets insert is wrapped in a DO $$ block guarded by `if not exists (select 1 from storage.buckets where id = 'verification-docs')`.
SQL is well-formed: sqlparse produces 26+ non-empty statements, $$ delimiters are balanced (16 occurrences, even), parentheses balanced (after comment-stripping), and the file terminates on `$$;`.

Flutter side is unaffected: lib/repositories/admin_repository.dart only calls admin_* RPCs by name (admin_dashboard_metrics, admin_user_summary, admin_suspend_user, admin_unsuspend_user, admin_soft_delete_user, admin_restore_user, admin_resolve_report, admin_dismiss_report, admin_set_verification). Grep across lib/ found zero `.rpc('is_admin'...)` calls — the only mention of `is_admin` is a comment on admin_repository.dart:4.

`flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos` on /app/nextchapter_audit/src: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 77 info-level deprecations (withOpacity → withValues, anonKey → publishableKey, RadioGroup migration, FormField initialValue rename). User has explicitly chosen to ignore these.

Note: This was file-level verification only. No Postgres / Docker is available in this environment, so the migration was not executed against a real database. The agent-to-agent note explicitly accepts file-level + flutter analyze verification as sufficient for this task.

success_rate.frontend: N/A (frontend testing was explicitly skipped per request; flutter analyze: 0 errors / 0 warnings / 77 accepted info deprecations)

test_credentials: Not applicable — no DB / API access required for this task.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The fix in 006_b8_verification.sql line 33 (`drop function if exists public.is_admin();`) is verified correct at the file level. Re-running pytest /app/backend/tests/test_migration_006_b8_verification.py will re-validate. If a Postgres instance becomes available, the natural next step is to actually execute 001→006 against a clean DB. All 17 file-level checks pass; the user can safely paste 006_b8_verification.sql into the Supabase SQL Editor.
rca of the issue: Root cause: migration 001 defined `public.is_admin(uid uuid default auth.uid())` (1-arg with default), and migration 005 later defined `public.is_admin()` (0-arg). Both signatures match a zero-argument call from RLS policies and RPC bodies, so PostgreSQL raises `42725: function public.is_admin() is not unique`. Mitigation (now applied at line 33 of 006_b8_verification.sql): `drop function if exists public.is_admin();` removes only the no-arg variant (Postgres dispatches drops by exact signature), leaving 001's 1-arg-with-default version. Subsequent no-arg call sites resolve uniquely to it. Verified the drop appears BEFORE any DDL inside 006 that calls is_admin() (first table/policy DDL is on line 36).
